seoTitle: Paraguay vs Panama Residency: Honest 2026 Comparison
seoDescription: Paraguay vs Panama residency compared: cost, timeline, tax and citizenship path — honestly, so you choose the country that actually fits your goals.

faqs.question: Is Paraguay or Panama cheaper for residency by investment?
faqs.answer: Paraguay, at every comparable stage. Its lowest route (SUACE company formation) starts at USD 70,000, against Panama's lowest permanent-residency-eligible threshold of USD 200,000 (Friendly Nations Visa). Even matched at USD 200,000, Paraguay grants direct permanent residency while Panama's Friendly Nations route requires a two-year provisional stage first.

faqs.question: Which country gives citizenship faster, Paraguay or Panama?
faqs.answer: Paraguay. Naturalisation eligibility opens after three years of permanent residence (Constitution, Art. 148), and permanent residency itself is typically granted within months of a qualifying investment. Panama's naturalisation window opens after five years of permanent residence specifically — provisional years don't count — making its cheaper Friendly Nations track roughly 7.5–8 years to citizenship eligibility overall, against Paraguay's 3–4 years.

faqs.question: Are both Paraguay and Panama really territorial tax systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, genuinely. In both, foreign-source income is generally outside the scope of local income tax, assessed on individual facts. They differ on local rates (Paraguay: 10% flat corporate, 8–10% progressive personal; Panama: 25% corporate, up to 25% personal) and on VAT (Paraguay 10%, Panama 7%). Neither system overrides your home country's own tax residency rules — see Paraguay's tax residency page for the full caveats.

faqs.question: Does Panama allow dual citizenship the way Paraguay does?
faqs.answer: Not as cleanly. Panama's naturalisation process includes a formal oath renouncing prior citizenship, with a narrower carve-out for Spanish and other Latin American nationals; in practice the oath is rarely enforced against applicants who don't formally renounce in their home country, but it remains a real legal step. Paraguay recognises dual nationality in defined circumstances, without an equivalent renunciation requirement.

faqs.question: Can I qualify for direct permanent residency in Panama the way I can in Paraguay?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only through Panama's Qualified Investor Visa, at USD 300,000 in real estate (rising to USD 500,000 after 15 October 2026), USD 500,000 in securities, or USD 750,000 in a fixed bank deposit. Panama's lower-cost Friendly Nations Visa, at USD 200,000, requires two years of provisional residency first — every Paraguay route under Resolution N° 0283/2026 skips that stage entirely.

faqs.question: Should I choose Paraguay or Panama for a Plan B?
faqs.answer: It depends on what you're optimising for. Paraguay wins on cost, speed to permanent residency, speed to citizenship, and maintenance burden. Panama wins on currency stability (the US dollar), depth of existing expat infrastructure, and regional business connectivity, particularly above the USD 300,000 investment level. We work exclusively on the Paraguay side, and we'll tell a prospective client plainly if their goals actually point toward Panama, or elsewhere, instead.

blocks.h1: Paraguay vs Panama residency: an honest 2026 comparison.
blocks.lead: Both countries run genuinely territorial tax systems and legitimate investment-linked residency. Paraguay is the more capital-efficient route — from USD 70,000, against Panama's USD 200,000 minimum — and reaches permanent residency, then citizenship, faster in nearly every comparable scenario. Panama is the more built-out, more internationally connected base. Neither is a “hack.” We only sell Paraguay — which is exactly why we've tried to be precise about where Panama is the better call.

stats.label: Paraguay's lowest route, vs Panama's $200K minimum
Both tax systems — the details differ

blocks.bgAlt: Financial district in Asunción, Paraguay — Paraguay vs Panama residency comparison

blocks.heading: Paraguay and Panama, side by side.

paragraphs.text: Most comparison content in this niche is written by whoever sells one of the two countries. This page is written by a firm that only sells Paraguay — which is exactly why we've tried to be precise about where that is, and isn't, the right call.

table.label: Legal basis
table.value:
Paraguay: Resolution N° 0283/2026 (MIC), four routes, plus a separate film pathway under Ley N° 6106/2018.
Panama: Friendly Nations Visa (executive decree) and the Qualified Investor Visa (“Golden Visa”).

table.label: Minimum investment
table.value:
Paraguay: USD 70,000 (SUACE company) up to USD 200,000 (real estate / financial instruments); USD 40,000 film contribution on a separate track.
Panama: USD 200,000 (Friendly Nations, real estate or bank deposit) or USD 300,000 (Qualified Investor, real estate — rising to USD 500,000 after 15 October 2026).

table.label: Residency granted
table.value:
Paraguay: Direct permanent residency — no temporary stage, under any of the four Resolution 0283/2026 routes.
Panama: Friendly Nations grants a 2-year provisional residency first, with permanent residency applied for afterward; Qualified Investor grants direct permanent residency.

table.label: Time to permanent residency
table.value:
Paraguay: Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) in ~5 business days; permanent residency itself typically several months.
Panama: Friendly Nations runs ~2.5–3 years total; Qualified Investor can process in as little as ~30 days once filed.

table.label: Maintenance requirement
table.value:
Paraguay: At least one visit every 3 years.
Panama: At least one visit every 2 years (over 24 hours); status can be suspended, and residency is automatically cancelled after 6 years' absence.

table.label: Tax system
table.value:
Paraguay: Territorial — foreign-source income generally outside Paraguayan income tax (Ley N° 6380/2019).
Panama: Territorial — foreign-source income generally exempt from Panamanian income tax.

table.label: Local personal tax rate
table.value:
Paraguay: 8% / 9% / 10% progressive on Paraguay-source income; exemption below ~USD 12,000/year.
Panama: 0% up to USD 11,000; 15% from USD 11,001–50,000; 25% above USD 50,000, on Panama-source income.

table.label: Corporate tax rate
table.value:
Paraguay: 10% flat (IRE).
Panama: 25% (5–10% in select economic zones).

table.label: VAT
table.value:
Paraguay: 10%.
Panama: 7% (ITBMS).

table.label: Time to citizenship eligibility
table.value:
Paraguay: 3 years of permanent residence (Constitution, Art. 148).
Panama: 5 years of permanent residence — provisional years do not count.

table.label: Naturalisation requirements
table.value:
Paraguay: Language, civics and genuine-ties (arraigo) requirements.
Panama: Spanish proficiency, civics knowledge, and an oath renouncing prior citizenship (rarely enforced abroad in practice).

table.label: Dual citizenship
table.value:
Paraguay: Recognised in defined circumstances.
Panama: Not formally recognised for most naturalised citizens, with a narrower carve-out for Spanish and Latin American nationals.

table.label: Currency
table.value:
Paraguay: Guaraní (floating); dollar-denominated real estate deals common.
Panama: US dollar is legal tender (the “balboa” is pegged 1:1 and dollars circulate directly).

asideNotice.title: Both are legitimate
asideNotice.body: Neither country's territorial tax system is a marketing trick — both are genuinely codified, source-based regimes. The difference is structural: how many stages stand between your investment and a permanent card, and how fast each path reaches citizenship.

blocks.heading: Where Panama serves you better.
blocks.lead: We'd be doing readers a disservice, and undermining our own credibility, if we pretended Panama has no case. For a specific kind of buyer, it's a stronger one than Paraguay.

cards.title: The US dollar, daily
cards.text: Panama uses the US dollar directly as legal tender — no guaraní to think about, no floating exchange rate to hedge on rent or salary. For an American especially, that removes a layer of friction Paraguay doesn't.

cards.title: A larger, more mature expat base
cards.text: Decades of established international schools, private healthcare networks, retiree communities (Boquete, Coronado, Panama City) and English-speaking service infrastructure Paraguay hasn't yet built to the same depth.

cards.title: A global logistics & business hub
cards.text: The Panama Canal, Tocumen International Airport's connectivity, and Panama's position as a re-export and banking centre are genuine, structural advantages for an international business moving people, goods or capital.

cards.title: Deeper real estate inventory
cards.text: Panama's luxury and coastal market is deeper and more mature than Paraguay's — more product, more resale liquidity, more established developers with decades of delivery history.

cards.title: Optionality of route
cards.text: The Qualified Investor Visa offers three qualifying-asset classes at different price points, plus the lower-cost Friendly Nations track — a broader range of structuring options at the higher end.

blocks.pull: If those points describe your priorities more than cost or speed to citizenship, Panama is a legitimate call — and we'd say so directly.

blocks.heading: Where Paraguay wins.
blocks.lead: For the buyer prioritising capital efficiency, simplicity, and the shortest honest path to a second passport, Paraguay's advantages are concrete rather than promotional.

cards.title: A materially lower entry point
cards.text: Paraguay's lowest qualifying route — the SUACE company investment — starts at USD 70,000, less than half of Panama's lowest permanent-residency threshold. Even Paraguay's real estate route at USD 200,000 matches Panama's Friendly Nations minimum while delivering direct permanent residency, not a two-year provisional stage first.

cards.title: Direct permanent residency, every route
cards.text: All four Resolution N° 0283/2026 routes — and the separate film pathway — bypass a temporary-residency stage entirely. Only Panama's pricier Qualified Investor track matches Paraguay on directness, at USD 300,000–500,000.

cards.title: A faster path to citizenship
cards.text: Paraguay's constitutional naturalisation window opens after 3 years of permanent residence. Panama's opens after 5 — and only permanent years count, so a Friendly Nations applicant is realistically 7.5–8 years from citizenship eligibility, against Paraguay's roughly 3–4.

cards.title: A lighter maintenance obligation
cards.text: One visit every three years, versus Panama's every two — a genuine difference for an internationally mobile family that isn't relocating full-time.

cards.title: Dual citizenship recognised, not fought
cards.text: Panama's naturalisation includes a formal oath renouncing prior citizenship — a real legal step. Paraguay recognises dual nationality in defined circumstances, a cleaner position for families who want to add a passport, not choose between them.

cards.title: Lower cost, investment-grade momentum
cards.text: Paraguay's rates (10% flat corporate, 8–10% progressive personal) sit below Panama's (25% corporate, up to 25% personal on local income), with a lower day-to-day cost of living. Paraguay also reached investment grade with Moody's (2024) and S&P (2025), posting some of the strongest real GDP growth in the region. See Paraguay's tax residency and territorial tax system for the full, caveated breakdown.

blocks.heading: Cost and investment thresholds, compared directly.

paragraphs.text: The headline number is only part of the picture. Paraguay's routes span a genuinely wide range, and the honest comparison depends on which Paraguay route you'd actually choose against which Panama route.

table.label: Paraguay — Film & audiovisual
table.value: USD 40,000 contribution (+USD 80,000 show of funds) — nothing recoverable, a contribution to an approved production.

table.label: Paraguay — SUACE company
table.value: USD 70,000 — an active, capitalised Paraguayan company.

table.label: Paraguay — Approved tourism project
table.value: USD 150,000 — a stake in an approved tourism development.

table.label: Paraguay — Real estate
table.value: USD 200,000 — title to income-generating property.

table.label: Paraguay — Financial instruments
table.value: USD 200,000, 2-year hold — regulated instruments, held in your name.

table.label: Panama — Friendly Nations (real estate or deposit)
table.value: USD 200,000 — property, or a 3-year fixed bank deposit.

table.label: Panama — Qualified Investor (real estate)
table.value: USD 300,000 until 15 Oct 2026, then USD 500,000 — property, held minimum 5 years.

table.label: Panama — Qualified Investor (securities)
table.value: USD 500,000 — Panamanian-listed securities via licensed broker.

table.label: Panama — Qualified Investor (bank deposit)
table.value: USD 750,000, 5-year term — a Panamanian fixed-term deposit.

notice.title: The real price of “cheaper”
notice.body: At every matching price point, Paraguay's route reaches direct permanent residency; Panama's cheapest matching route (Friendly Nations) does not — the true “price” of that cheaper option includes roughly two extra years in provisional status. Government processing fees differ too: Panama's Friendly Nations Visa carries fixed, non-refundable government charges of roughly USD 1,050 per applicant, separate from the qualifying investment — comparable in kind to Paraguay's own Migraciones tariffs, detailed on our full Paraguay cost breakdown.

blocks.imageAlt: Legal and business formation paperwork — investment thresholds compared

blocks.heading: Two paths, a different number of stages.

paragraphs.text: The structural difference between the two countries is how many stages stand between “invest” and “hold a permanent card.”

If speed to a permanent card matters and budget is constrained to the USD 150,000–250,000 range, Paraguay is structurally faster than Panama's comparable-price option. If budget can stretch past USD 300,000, the two countries converge on timeline, and the decision shifts to the other factors on this page.

notice.title: No interim stage in Paraguay
notice.body: Paraguay's path is a single, direct line: choose a route, make the qualifying investment, receive the CIE within five business days of a complete file, then progress permanent residency with the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones — typically resolved within several months. See the full requirements and document checklist for the complete sequence.

items.title: Paraguay — direct
items.text: Qualifying investment → CIE (~5 business days) → permanent residency (several months). No interim temporary-residency stage.

items.title: Panama — Friendly Nations Visa
items.text: Provisional residency first (~4–6 months to approve), held two years, then a separate permanent-residency application (another 4–6 months). Roughly 2.5–3 years total.

items.title: Panama — Qualified Investor Visa
items.text: Grants permanent residency directly, with processing that can run in as little as 30 days once the file is complete — Panama's route that genuinely competes with Paraguay on speed, at a materially higher investment threshold.

blocks.heading: Tax systems, compared honestly.
blocks.lead: Both countries are genuinely territorial — this is not one country marketing “0% tax” more aggressively while running a different system underneath. Where they differ is what happens to local income and activity.

cards.title: Local personal income
cards.text: Paraguay taxes Paraguay-source personal-services income progressively at 8%, 9% or 10%, with an exemption below roughly USD 12,000 a year. Panama exempts the first USD 11,000, then taxes 15% up to USD 50,000 and 25% above it — a meaningfully steeper local top rate.

cards.title: Corporate income
cards.text: Paraguay's flat 10% corporate rate (IRE) is among the lowest in the hemisphere; Panama's standard 25% is closer to US and European norms, though Panama offers reduced 5–10% rates inside specific economic zones.

cards.title: VAT
cards.text: Panama's ITBMS runs a 7% standard rate against Paraguay's 10% — a genuine Panama advantage on local consumption.

cards.title: What territorial tax doesn't do, in either country
cards.text: Neither country can switch off your home country's tax rules. A US citizen remains taxed on worldwide income regardless of residency in either, under citizenship-based taxation; UK, Canadian, Australian and most European nationals must still satisfy their own home-country exit tests. See Paraguay tax residency, explained for the full, caveated picture on the Paraguay side.

blocks.pull: Neither this page nor our tax residency page is tax advice for either jurisdiction — have your specific facts reviewed by qualified counsel in both countries before committing capital.

paragraphs.text: This is where the two paths diverge most for the family thinking three moves ahead, not just one. Paraguay's Constitution (Art. 148) opens naturalisation eligibility after three years of permanent residence, subject to language, civics and genuine-ties requirements — the clock starts the moment permanent status is granted, which itself arrives quickly. Dual citizenship is recognised in defined circumstances, so most applicants aren't forced to choose between passports.

Panama's naturalisation window opens after five years, but only years spent as a permanent resident count — provisional years on the Friendly Nations track don't. For that route, realistic total time from first filing to citizenship eligibility runs closer to 7.5–8 years. The Qualified Investor Visa, granting permanent residency directly, closes that gap to roughly five years from filing. Panama's process also involves a formal oath renouncing prior citizenship — rarely enforced against applicants who never formally renounce at home, and Panama grants a narrower dual-citizenship carve-out for Spanish and other Latin American nationals.

On raw travel mobility, the two passports are broadly comparable and both solidly mid-tier by global standards; recent Henley Passport Index data has ranked Paraguay's passport marginally ahead of Panama's on total visa-free-and-visa-on-arrival access, though rankings shift year to year and shouldn't be the deciding factor for either country. See Paraguay citizenship by naturalisation for the full timeline and requirements.

asideTable.label: Panama citizenship window
asideTable.value: 5 years of permanent residence — provisional years excluded

asideTable.label: Dual citizenship
asideTable.value: Paraguay: recognised in defined circumstances. Panama: narrower carve-out (Spain, Latin America)

blocks.heading: Which country actually fits you.
blocks.lead: Rather than declare a universal winner, here is the honest decision framework we walk clients through directly.

cards.title: Choose Paraguay if
cards.text: You want the lowest capital outlay for direct permanent residency, the fastest realistic path to a second citizenship, a lighter maintenance obligation, and you're comfortable being an early mover in a market that is investment-grade but still developing its expat infrastructure.

cards.title: Choose Panama's Qualified Investor Visa if
cards.text: Budget isn't the constraint, you want the US dollar as your daily currency and Panama's more mature infrastructure, and you can commit USD 300,000 or more — accepting that citizenship still takes roughly five years and involves a formal renunciation oath.

cards.title: Be cautious of Panama's Friendly Nations Visa if
cards.text: Cost is the main driver. At a comparable price point, it is structurally slower than any Paraguay route — two extra years in provisional status before permanent residency even begins, and closer to eight years total before citizenship eligibility.

cards.title: Consider both, sequentially, if
cards.text: You're building genuine regional optionality rather than a single Plan B. Some clients hold Paraguayan residency as the faster, lower-cost base and add Panama later, once budget allows, rather than treating the choice as exclusive.

blocks.eyebrow: If Paraguay is the right fit
blocks.heading: The route that matches your capital and objectives.
blocks.lead: Whichever route fits, we'll confirm — plainly, before anything is committed — whether Paraguay, a different route, or a different country entirely is the right call for your circumstances.

rungs.title: Eligible Real Estate
rungs.text: Qualifying income-generating property — a tangible, appreciating asset you hold and control.

rungs.title: Financial Instruments
rungs.text: A qualifying financial investment held for the required period — the passive, hands-off route.

rungs.title: Approved Touristic Projects
rungs.text: Investment into approved tourism developments — yield potential plus residency.

rungs.title: SUACE Business Investment
rungs.text: Establish and capitalise a Paraguayan company through the unified business system.

rungs.title: Film Cultural Investment
rungs.text: A qualifying contribution to Paraguayan audiovisual production — a separate, lower-cost pathway.

blocks.note: See Paraguay residency requirements and the full cost breakdown for the document set and true all-in cost behind each route above.

blocks.pull: The honest comparison, then the right call for you.
blocks.lead: Request information and we'll confirm — plainly, before anything is committed — whether Paraguay, a different route, or a different country is the right fit.
